question: Can AI agents actually use your product?
lede: AXArena is a neutral, open-source agent usability benchmark that helps developers understand which products are genuinely agent-friendly—especially across APIs, CLIs, and discoverability. We quantify agent experience; we do not judge whether a product is good or bad.

findings.title: Reliability changes the ordering.
findings.body: The shared-task score tells us whether a vendor can succeed; three-trial consistency shows whether that success is dependable.

findings.title: Discoverability is a separate product property.
findings.body: A vendor can expose strong execution paths while agents still struggle to find the canonical endpoint or auth scheme. Discovery is published beside usability and never changes rank.

findings.title: Simple read-back tasks form the common floor.
findings.body: Metadata inspection and record lifecycle tasks provide a comparable core while product-specific capabilities remain visible through applicability coverage.

limitations: The checked-in values are illustrative until the frozen production export replaces this draft fixture.
AXArena Database v1 scores API and CLI only; SDK and MCP remain outside the benchmark-of-record.
Backup, CDC, and integrity are research tasks and do not enter the benchmark.
Sandbox plans, product changes, harness versions, and documentation changes can affect future reruns.
independence: Vendors do not write their own benchmark tasks or buy placement.
Factual corrections are welcome; editorial control and score suppression are not offered.
Score-changing corrections and reruns belong in the public changelog.
